title: tithymalus sahendi
intro: Tithymalus sahendi, also known as Euphorbia sahendi, is a succulent plant species native to Iran, characterized by its unique morphology and medicinal properties. The plant contains toxic compounds, such as latex and sap, which have been used in traditional medicine for their analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Consuming the plant can provide health benefits like pain relief, wound healing, and protection against infections, but it should be handled with caution due to its toxicity. In herbal preparations, Tithymalus sahendi is often used in the form of extracts or ointments, which are applied topically to alleviate skin conditions and other health issues.
